events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: sawcut joints late (random cracking - FD). This is a defect in concrete finishing/curing. Sawcutting contraction joints too late leads to random cracking. This is non-compliant/installed-defective. Decision fail. Risk medium/high. Work stage rough-complete. Percent complete maybe 80. Findings: late sawcutting caused random cracking. Actions: evaluate cracks, repair per engineer, revise joint sawcutting timing. References ACI 302, 308.

  "findings": [
    "Contraction joints sawcut too late, resulting in random cracking of the slab",
    "Random cracking indicates uncontrolled shrinkage cracks formed before joints were activated",
    "Joint sawcutting timing did not meet ACI guidance for early-age cutting window"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Map and document all random cracks for engineering evaluation",
    "Obtain repair/remediation direction from engineer (e.g., epoxy injection, routing/sealing) before closeout",
    "Review and correct sawcutting schedule/timing on remaining placements to cut within the early-age window"
  ],
  "rationale": "Late sawcutting of contraction joints allowed shrinkage stresses to relieve as random cracking rather than at controlled joints, a defective and non-compliant condition under ACI 302/308 finishing and curing practice.",
  "references": ["ACI 302", "ACI 308"]
}
